objective: Repair `imobench/answerbench_v2.csv` so `imo-bench-algebra-036` has the reported short answer `$Y(x)=A+B/x-x$` in its own Short Answer column and again has exactly the six columns declared by the dataset header.
completion_criteria: Parse the CSV with a standards-compliant reader, assert that every row has six columns, assert the exact short-answer field for `imo-bench-algebra-036`, and pass any repository checks. The current main branch was independently reproduced as a five-column row.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Repeat when the authoritative status changes materially or the baseline is explicitly invalidated.

synthesis_summary: Upstream status was refreshed without triggering a stop condition. The exact one-byte repair is materialized at imobench/answerbench_v2.csv and passes the full verification contract plus negative controls. No external action was taken.

established_facts.claim: The pinned upstream CSV has exactly 400 data records under ordinary parsing, with width counts {5: 1, 6: 399}; the width-five record is algebra-036.
A quote inserted at byte offset 13,618 restores six fields on every record and exact target Short Answer $Y(x)=A+\frac{B}{x}-x$.

strategies.mechanism: Add a generic standards-library test that parses every maintained benchmark CSV and checks each data-row width against its header, with a target-literal regression assertion for issue #13.
strategies.hypothesis: A generic guard would fail uniquely on the pinned baseline and prevent similar malformed-row regressions.
strategies.discriminating_test: Run it on the pinned tree and repaired tree; require one failure before and zero after.
strategies.rationale: Potentially reusable, but lower-value than the already complete minimal fix unless requested upstream.
strategies.title: Add a generic standards-library test that parses every maintained benchmark CSV and checks each data-row width against its header, with a target-literal regression assertion for issue #13.

strategies.family: CSV structural validation and minimal-patch certification
strategies.title: Refresh upstream status, materialize the isolated closing quote in imobench/answerbench_v2.csv, and certify strict schema, exact target value, and one-byte-only equality against the pinned source.
strategies.mechanism: A standards-library parser checks every record; the issue-supplied token fixes the boundary; an independently written target-aware checker enforces exact fields and baseline-plus-one-byte equality.
strategies.hypothesis: Against unchanged upstream commit 96fa6c4cc3a9bb7450ee7b6773b659d3a030dace, the materialized file is exactly the derived one-byte quote insertion and restores six strict-parsed columns plus the exact algebra-036 Short Answer.
strategies.discriminating_test: Run the materialized candidate through the strict baseline/control harness and the separate checker with --reference; require baseline rejection, candidate acceptance, exact-byte equality, and rejection of wrong-answer and collateral-edit controls.
strategies.blocker: External acceptance requires human-owner authorization, Google CLA readiness, and upstream maintainer review.
strategies.reopen_condition: Refresh or redirect if upstream main, issue status, competing PR status, or target bytes change.
strategies.reopen_evidence: Upstream main and issue/PR status were refreshed on 2026-07-22 and remained compatible with the pinned baseline.

open_leads.description: Apply and verify the isolated one-byte patch against a freshly resolved upstream head.
open_leads.rationale: It is the cheapest path from verified baseline to the requested dataset correction.
open_leads.next_experiment: Run git ls-remote for upstream main, refresh if needed, then invoke the checker with --reference on the materialized candidate.

open_leads.description: Seek upstream acceptance after owner authorization.

tactical_memory.current_bottleneck: Technical uncertainty is removed; owner approval, CLA readiness, and upstream review are now limiting.

failure_signatures.signature: An unclosed quoted Problem field can be tolerated as a five-field record by ordinary parsing but rejected as malformed by strict parsing; semantic-only repair checks can miss unrelated edits.
Semantic-only validation accepts a repaired file containing an unrelated equal-length problem-ID mutation; exact reference comparison is mandatory.

decision_history.prediction: Ordinary parsing would show 399 six-field and one five-field row; a one-byte candidate would yield 400 six-field rows and pass all controls.
decision_history.observation: The width prediction and all candidate controls passed; strict parsing of the malformed baseline raised a CSV syntax error while strict parsing of the repair passed.
decision_history.surprise: The initial strict-parser run failed earlier than the predicted width count, providing a stronger syntax-level diagnostic; the harness was revised to preserve both tolerant-width and strict-syntax signals.
decision_history.next_discriminator: Resolve upstream main; if unchanged, materialize the one-byte candidate and run the narrow checker with the pinned source as --reference.

decision_history.prediction: The unchanged baseline would fail, while the materialized one-byte repair would pass all structural, semantic, and exact-diff gates.
decision_history.observation: All predicted signals occurred; the candidate hash equals the previously derived repair hash, and all negative controls behaved as required.
decision_history.next_discriminator: Immediately before any authorized PR, refresh upstream main, issue state, and competing PRs; rerun the exact-diff checker if unchanged.

reduction_ledger.remaining_unknown: Arbitrary multi-byte rewrites were not classified; external maintainer acceptance is not computationally decidable here.
reduction_ledger.next_bulk_elimination: None; the technical edit space is exhausted.

prior_art_decisions.exact_delta: Issue #13 reports the merged fields. This epoch adds a hash-bound full-file reproduction, identifies the exact one-byte edit, supplies an exact-diff checker, and tests strict-parser, wrong-answer, and unrelated-edit controls.
prior_art_decisions.duplicate_risk: A fix could appear after the timestamp, exist in an unindexed fork, or be submitted under wording absent from the two repository PR searches.
prior_art_decisions.comparison_test: Before any patch or PR, resolve current main and rerun repository issue/PR searches for answerbench_v2 and imo-bench-algebra-036.
